title: Kubernetes HorizontalPodAutoscaler Scaling Event Frequency, Flapping, and Cooldown Throttling
exclusions: Does not replace UC-3.2.17 workload-level maxReplicas saturation and FailedGet metric-fetch dysfunction, UC-3.2.24 HorizontalPodAutoscaler decision introspection against metrics APIs when catalogued as a distinct UC, UC-3.2.46 Cluster Autoscaler and Karpenter node scale-out failure with Pending backlog, UC-3.2.6 Deployment rollout condition analytics, or UC-3.2.4 namespace ResourceQuota admission denial. Does not emit invoices; est_extra_cost_usd_24h is a heuristic planning field only.
evidence: Saved search uc_3_2_11_hpa_scaling_activity with fifteen-minute or five-minute schedule; hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv versioned in git; weekly CSV export of the alert table to a restricted evidence index with kube-state-metrics chart version for auditors.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab namespace, configure an HPA with tight averageUtilization against a workload whose CPU oscillates on a short period until five-minute kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_current_replicas buckets show at least six transitions within one hour, confirm kube:events SuccessfulRescale for the same HorizontalPodAutoscaler, execute uc_3_2_11_hpa_scaling_activity, and expect flap_heavy equal to one with severity at least high when hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv marks production tier.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Remove oscillating load, confirm current and desired replica series hold flat across twelve consecutive five-minute buckets with no new SuccessfulRescale events, and verify the saved search emits no qualifying rows for that HorizontalPodAutoscaler across four consecutive intervals.

dataSources: index=k8s_metrics or index=k8s with sourcetype=prometheus:scrape:metrics or kube:objects:metrics carrying kube-state-metrics kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_current_replicas, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_desired_replicas, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_min_replicas, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_max_replicas, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_condition with condition and status labels, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_target_metric when enabled; index=k8s_events or index=k8s with sourcetype=kube:events for HorizontalPodAutoscaler involvedObject kinds with reason SuccessfulRescale; Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector prometheus and kubernetes events receivers; governance lookup hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv keyed on cluster, namespace, horizontalpodautoscaler for owner_team, workload_tier, cluster_hourly_cost_usd, expected_stabilize_sec, and behavior_scaleup_period_sec.
app: kube-state-metrics (upstream Deployment and ClusterRole) with HorizontalPodAutoscaler informers enabled; Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Kubernetes with prometheus_simple or prometheus receiver and splunk_hec exporter; Splunk Enterprise 9.2+ or Splunk Cloud search tier for scheduled correlation searches.
spl:
`comment("UC-3.2.11 HPA scaling activity axis: replica and desired-replica transition rate, SuccessfulRescale corroboration, status conditions, spec context, burst gates (>=6 transitions in 1h or >=30 in 24h). Tunables: earliest=-24h@h latest=@m; bucket=5m; indexes k8s_metrics k8s_events")`
| eval uc_join="3211"
| join type=left uc_join [
| tstats count AS perf_lane_ticks FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE (nodename=Performance.CPU OR nodename=Performance.Memory) earliest=-24h@h latest=now
| eval uc_join="3211" ]
| fields - uc_join perf_lane_ticks
| multisearch [
    [ search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-24h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "horizontalpodautoscaler=\"(?<hpa_rx>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\"(?<ns_rx>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\s(?<mval>[0-9.eE+-]+)\s*$"
      | eval hpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(horizontalpodautoscaler, hpa_rx, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | where like(mn,"%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_current_replicas%") OR like(mn,"%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_desired_replicas%")
      | eval cur_r=if(like(mn,"%status_current_replicas%"), tonumber(mval, 10), null())
      | eval des_r=if(like(mn,"%status_desired_replicas%"), tonumber(mval, 10), null())
      | bin _time span=5m aligntime=@h
      | stats latest(cur_r) AS current_replicas latest(des_r) AS desired_replicas BY cluster namespace hpa _time
      | sort 0 cluster namespace hpa + _time
      | streamstats current=f global=f last(current_replicas) AS prev_cr last(desired_replicas) AS prev_dr BY cluster namespace hpa
      | eval scale_evt=if((isnotnull(prev_cr) AND current_replicas!=prev_cr) OR (isnotnull(prev_dr) AND desired_replicas!=prev_dr), 1, 0)
      | eval dir=case(isnull(prev_cr), "na", current_replicas>prev_cr OR (isnotnull(desired_replicas) AND isnotnull(prev_dr) AND desired_replicas>prev_dr), "up", current_replicas<prev_cr OR (isnotnull(desired_replicas) AND isnotnull(prev_dr) AND desired_replicas<prev_dr), "down", true(), "flat")
      | stats sum(scale_evt) AS scale_events_24h sum(eval(if(_time>=relative_time(now(),"-1h"), scale_evt, 0))) AS scale_events_1h values(dir) AS dir_turns BY cluster namespace hpa ]
    [ search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-24h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "horizontalpodautoscaler=\"(?<hpa_rx>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\"(?<ns_rx>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "condition=\"(?<cond_l>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "status=\"(?<stat_l>[^\"]+)\""
      | where like(mn,"%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_condition%")
      | eval hpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(horizontalpodautoscaler, hpa_rx, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval cond_pair=printf("%s=%s", cond_l, stat_l)
      | stats values(cond_pair) AS cond_values max(_time) AS cond_last_ts BY cluster namespace hpa ]
    [ search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-24h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "horizontalpodautoscaler=\"(?<hpa_rx>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\"(?<ns_rx>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\s(?<mval>[0-9.eE+-]+)\s*$"
      | where like(mn,"%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_min_replicas%") OR like(mn,"%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_max_replicas%") OR like(mn,"%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_target_metric%")
      | eval hpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(horizontalpodautoscaler, hpa_rx, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval min_r=if(like(mn,"%spec_min_replicas%"), tonumber(mval, 10), null())
      | eval max_r=if(like(mn,"%spec_max_replicas%"), tonumber(mval, 10), null())
      | eval tgt_line=if(like(mn,"%spec_target_metric%"), _raw, null())
      | stats latest(min_r) AS min_replicas latest(max_r) AS max_replicas values(tgt_line) AS target_metric_raw BY cluster namespace hpa ]
    [ search (index=k8s_events OR index=k8s) sourcetype="kube:events" earliest=-24h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, source_cluster, ""))))
      | eval reason=trim(toString(coalesce(reason, Reason, "")))
      | eval kind=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject.kind, `involvedObject.kind`, ""))))
      | eval hpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject.name, involvedObject_name, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metadata.namespace, namespace, ""))))
      | where kind="horizontalpodautoscaler" AND reason="SuccessfulRescale"
      | stats count AS successful_rescale_24h BY cluster namespace hpa ]
| eval merge_key=cluster."|".namespace."|".hpa
| stats max(scale_events_24h) AS scale_events_24h max(scale_events_1h) AS scale_events_1h values(dir_turns) AS dir_turns values(cond_values) AS cond_values max(cond_last_ts) AS cond_last_ts max(min_replicas) AS min_replicas max(max_replicas) AS max_replicas values(target_metric_raw) AS target_metric_raw max(successful_rescale_24h) AS successful_rescale_24h BY merge_key
| rex field=merge_key "^(?<cluster>[^|]+)\|(?<namespace>[^|]+)\|(?<hpa>[^|]+)$"
| eval cond_blob=mvjoin(mvdedup(cond_values), " | ")
| eval flap_heavy=if(scale_events_1h>=6 OR scale_events_24h>=30, 1, 0)
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace hpa [
    | inputlookup hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(namespace)))
      | eval hpa=lower(trim(toString(horizontalpodautoscaler)))
      | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team, squad, platform_team, "")))
      | eval workload_tier=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(workload_tier, tier, env_tier, "dev"))))
      | eval cluster_hourly_cost_usd=tonumber(tostring(cluster_hourly_cost_usd), 10)
      | eval expected_stabilize_sec=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_stabilize_sec, "300")), 10)
      | eval behavior_scaleup_period_sec=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(behavior_scaleup_period_sec, "60")), 10)
      | fields cluster namespace hpa owner_team workload_tier cluster_hourly_cost_usd expected_stabilize_sec behavior_scaleup_period_sec ]
| fillnull value=0 flap_heavy successful_rescale_24h scale_events_1h scale_events_24h
| fillnull value="unassigned" owner_team
| fillnull value="dev" workload_tier
| fillnull value=0.0 cluster_hourly_cost_usd
| fillnull value=300 expected_stabilize_sec
| fillnull value=60 behavior_scaleup_period_sec
| eval est_extra_rep_hours=round(scale_events_24h*0.25, 3)
| eval est_extra_cost_usd_24h=round(est_extra_rep_hours*cluster_hourly_cost_usd, 2)
| eval aggressive_policy_hint=if(behavior_scaleup_period_sec<=15 AND scale_events_1h>=3, 1, if(expected_stabilize_sec<=60 AND scale_events_24h>=18, 1, 0))
| eventstats max(scale_events_24h) AS ns_peak_scale_activity BY namespace
| eval scaling_active_false=if(match(lower(cond_blob), "scalingactive=false"), 1, 0)
| eval severity=case(flap_heavy==1 AND match(workload_tier, "prod|production|gold|tier1|tier-1"), "critical", flap_heavy==1, "high", aggressive_policy_hint==1 AND scale_events_24h>=12, "high", scale_events_24h>=20 OR scale_events_1h>=5, "medium", scaling_active_false==1 AND scale_events_24h>=8, "medium", true(), "low")
| where severity IN ("critical","high","medium")
| table cluster namespace hpa owner_team workload_tier scale_events_1h scale_events_24h successful_rescale_24h flap_heavy aggressive_policy_hint min_replicas max_replicas cond_blob target_metric_raw ns_peak_scale_activity est_extra_cost_usd_24h severity
description: Detects Kubernetes HorizontalPodAutoscaler scaling activity distinct from max-replica pinning alone, from metric-decision introspection alone, and from cluster node scale-out refusal: kube-state-metrics kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_current_replicas and kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_desired_replicas sampled into five-minute buckets feed streamstats transition counts, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_min_replicas, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_max_replicas, and kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_target_metric provide spec context, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_condition carries ScalingActive, AbleToScale, and ScalingLimited truth, kube:events reason SuccessfulRescale corroborates controller-acknowledged rescales, multisearch fan-in avoids single-arm blind spots, inputlookup hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv supplies stabilization and policy expectations plus cluster_hourly_cost_usd for directional cost overlays, eventstats surfaces per-namespace flap peaks, and case() assigns severity when burst gates exceed six transitions in one hour or thirty in twenty-four hours or when aggressive policy hints disagree with observed churn. UC-3.2.17 covers saturation and FailedGet planes. UC-3.2.24 covers decision introspection. UC-3.2.46 covers Cluster Autoscaler scale-out failure.
value: Mean time to repair improves when platform and application teams see one row that names cluster, namespace, scaler, one-hour and twenty-four hour transition counts, SuccessfulRescale corroboration, flap burst flags, aggressive cooldown hints, min and max replica bounds, condition text, namespace heat peaks, directional cost estimate, and severity without manually diffing kubectl, metrics explorers, and FinOps spreadsheets. Customer impact drops because replica thrash is caught before load balancers oscillate and before autoscalers fight their own stabilization windows. Capacity reviews gain evidence for widening behavior.scaleUp.stabilizationWindowSeconds, lengthening behavior.scaleDown.stabilizationWindowSeconds, relaxing behavior.scaleUp.policies periodSeconds floors, or fixing vendor metric pipelines before game-day traffic multiplies transition noise.
implementation: Scrape kube-state-metrics HPA replica, condition, and spec metric families into k8s_metrics, ship HorizontalPodAutoscaler kube:events including SuccessfulRescale into k8s_events, publish hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv with owner_team, workload_tier, cluster_hourly_cost_usd, expected_stabilize_sec, and behavior_scaleup_period_sec, save uc_3_2_11_hpa_scaling_activity on a fifteen-minute or five-minute schedule with earliest=-24h@h latest=now, route critical flap rows for production tiers, and validate in lab by forcing rapid metric oscillation against tight averageUtilization targets.
visualization: Heatmap of ns_peak_scale_activity by namespace; timechart of scale_events_1h; overlay SuccessfulRescale rate; table matching the closing SPL columns with drilldowns to kube:events and kube-state-metrics raw lines.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Application_State.state) AS app_state latest(Application_State.info) AS app_info FROM datamodel=Application_State WHERE nodename=Application_State (Application_State.app="kubernetes" OR Application_State.app="k8s" OR like(Application_State.app, "%kube%")) earliest=-24h@h latest=@h BY Application_State.dest
| rename Application_State.dest AS cim_host
| join type=left max=0 cim_host [
| tstats summariesonly=true avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load_pct avg(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_used_pct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance.CPU OR nodename=Performance.Memory earliest=-24h@h latest=@h BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS cim_host ]
| table cim_host app_state app_info cpu_load_pct mem_used_pct
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Application_State and Performance summaries on entities that represent Kubernetes nodes or management hosts so tstats overlays stay warm when correlating HPA transition storms with underlying saturation; retain at least thirty days for quarterly capacity reviews.
knownFalsePositives: Scheduled batch jobs that briefly push CPU or memory above target can legitimately trigger several up and down transitions around each run window; align alert schedules with job calendars or exclude batch namespaces via lookup flags. Autoscale-on-startup warm-up after deploys often produces a short burst of transitions while probes and caches settle; require sustained flap_heavy across multiple evaluation intervals before paging executives. Business-hours traffic crests and legitimate lunch-trough dips can produce civil up and down motion without customer harm; pair with golden signal dashboards before opening sev-one bridges. CronJob-style cyclical workloads can create repeating daily patterns that resemble flapping; use seasonality macros or baseline compares. Freshly deployed workloads calibrating requests and limits may oscillate until Vertical Pod Autoscaler or manual tuning stabilizes; suppress known onboarding namespaces for their first week when documented. KEDA cold-start bursts from scale-to-zero can spike transition counts during the first minutes of an event wave; cross-check ScaledObject cooldown before blaming classic HPA misconfiguration. Scrape-interval mismatch between kube-state-metrics and application metrics can alias sharp spikes into multi-bucket transitions; validate scrape alignment and recording rules. Custom-metric API outages or partial responses can make external metrics flap while CPU is flat; pivot to adapter health checks when ScalingActive toggles with sparse SuccessfulRescale lines. Very low minReplicas combined with bursty internet traffic can produce frequent scale-out edges that are economically intentional; review minReplicas policy before widening stabilization windows. Deliberate cluster cost optimization that uses aggressive scale-down policies may increase down transitions during off-hours; confirm FinOps-approved schedules before treating as defect. Node rotation or consolidation that reschedules many pods can create correlated rescales across HPAs that look like fleet-wide flapping; correlate with UC-3.2.46 timelines and node drain windows.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — open an episode when flap_heavy is critical on tier-1 HPAs; bind cluster, namespace, and scaler name as entity identifiers

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control with Kubernetes platform site reliability engineers, workload owners who tune HorizontalPodAutoscaler objects, FinOps partners who review incremental replica-hour burn, and observability engineers operating Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector plus kube-state-metrics across production and pre-production estates. This use case isolates the HPA scaling activity axis only: how often observable replica or desired-replica transitions occur per scaler, whether those transitions burst beyond sane stabilization policy, whether Kubernetes events with reason SuccessfulRescale corroborate metric-derived activity, and whether status conditions explain throttling versus healthy oscillation. UC-3.2.17 remains the maxReplicas saturation, metric-fetch failure, and dwell-at-ceiling plane; do not merge that detector here. UC-3.2.24, when present in your catalogue, remains decision introspection against metrics APIs rather than holistic event-rate analytics. UC-3.2.46 remains Cluster Autoscaler and Karpenter node scale-out refusal with Pending backlog; node provisioning refusal can starve HPAs but this UC never substitutes for that evidence. UC-3.2.6 remains Deployment rollout condition and ReplicaSet churn semantics without treating every replica change as autoscaler flapping.

Before you schedule the saved search, confirm four indexing paths are healthy. First, index=k8s_metrics ingests Prometheus text or normalized scrape events from kube-state-metrics that include kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_current_replicas, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_desired_replicas, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_min_replicas, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_max_replicas, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_condition with labels for condition type and status, and kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_target_metric lines that carry the declared target context for CPU, memory, or external metric kinds when your exporter version emits them. Second, index=k8s_events or a shared platform events index carries sourcetype kube:events with HorizontalPodAutoscaler involvedObject kinds and reason SuccessfulRescale so controller-acknowledged rescales can be counted beside scrape-derived transitions. Third, publish lookups/hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv with columns cluster, namespace, horizontalpodautoscaler, owner_team, workload_tier, cluster_hourly_cost_usd for rough FinOps overlays, expected_stabilize_sec mirroring the behavior.scaleUp.stabilizationWindowSeconds you intend to enforce per tier, and behavior_scaleup_period_sec mirroring the shortest behavior.scaleUp.policies periodSeconds you allow for production; optional notes may cite internal runbooks for cooldown tuning. Fourth, RBAC on collectors must allow listing watches on HorizontalPodAutoscaler objects in namespaces you monitor while keeping Secret reads out of forwarder ServiceAccounts unless policy demands broader access.

Risk framing for incident commanders: violent scaling activity often precedes customer-visible latency oscillations, noisy load balancers, and surprise cloud bills even when the scaler never pins at maxReplicas. Controllers respect stabilization windows; if your observed transition rate routinely exceeds what those windows should permit, someone may have set aggressive policies, metrics may alias across scrape intervals, or an external metric may be flapping. ScalingActive false, AbleToScale false, and ScalingLimited true still belong in the same row so responders see whether Kubernetes is refusing motion versus thrashing through motion.

Capacity and licensing: HPA series cardinality scales with namespace and object count. Preserve only necessary labels at scrape time after FinOps review. HEC tokens stay in vault with quarterly rotation. Legal review may require redacting internal metric names embedded in kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_target_metric raw lines when those strings echo proprietary queue names.

Governance: hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv refreshes weekly from the service catalog or Git repository that owns HorizontalPodAutoscaler manifests and behavior blocks. Gold-tier workloads get explicit rows so severity routing does not fall through to a generic platform queue during a revenue-impacting flap.

Training: teach responders to read kubectl describe horizontalpodautoscaler for behavior.scaleUp.stabilizationWindowSeconds, behavior.scaleDown.stabilizationWindowSeconds, and policies periodSeconds alongside Splunk rows, and to compare metrics-server or adapter endpoints when transition storms coincide with API hiccups.

Review cadence: quarterly replay one historical flap remediated by widening stabilization windows and another remediated by fixing a vendor metric outage, validating that this search still separates the two failure modes.

Hardware scope: Amazon EKS, Google GKE, Microsoft AKS, Red Hat OpenShift, VMware Tanzu, and self-managed Kubernetes where kube-state-metrics RBAC can list HorizontalPodAutoscaler objects cluster-wide; Arm and x86 worker fleets are in scope when Prometheus text lines remain compatible.

Differentiation recap: scaling event rate, burst gates, cooldown policy hints, and SuccessfulRescale corroboration—not max-replica dwell alone, not cluster autoscaler refusal, not Deployment rollout conditions.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Deploy kube-state-metrics with cluster-scoped RBAC that can list HorizontalPodAutoscaler objects. Point Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector prometheus receiver or prometheus_simple scrape jobs at the kube-state-metrics Service on port 8080 or 8443 depending on your chart, preserve horizontalpodautoscaler, namespace, and cluster labels through relabel_config blocks, and export to HEC into index=k8s_metrics with sourcetype prometheus:scrape:metrics. Mirror other gold Kubernetes use cases in this repository: bearer_token_file usage stays consistent with your security model, and honor_labels discipline prevents duplicate scrape collisions when both in-cluster Prometheus and OpenTelemetry hit the same endpoint without coordination.

ServiceMonitor-style reference:

apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: ServiceMonitor
metadata:
  name: kube-state-metrics-hpa-activity
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: kube-state-metrics
  endpoints:
    - port: http-metrics
      interval: 30s
      scrapeTimeout: 10s
      path: /metrics

Configure Kubernetes API events to land in index=k8s_events or index=k8s with sourcetype kube:events. SuccessfulRescale events must retain intact reason fields for the fourth multisearch arm.

OpenTelemetry Collector sketch:

receivers:
  prometheus/k8s_state:
    config:
      scrape_configs:
        - job_name: kube-state-metrics-hpa-activity
          kubernetes_sd_configs:
            - role: endpoints
          relabel_configs:
            - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_service_name]
              action: keep
              regex: kube-state-metrics
  k8s_events:
    auth_type: serviceAccount
    mode: watch
exporters:
  splunk_hec/metrics:
    token: ${SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN_K8S_METRICS}
    endpoint: https://splunk-hec.example:8088/services/collector/event
    index: k8s_metrics
    sourcetype: prometheus:scrape:metrics
  splunk_hec/events:
    token: ${SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN_K8S_EVENTS}
    endpoint: https://splunk-hec.example:8088/services/collector/event
    index: k8s_events
    sourcetype: kube:events
service:
  pipelines:
    metrics/ksm:
      receivers: [prometheus/k8s_state]
      exporters: [splunk_hec/metrics]
    logs/events:
      receivers: [k8s_events]
      exporters: [splunk_hec/events]

Publish lookups/hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv aligned to join keys in Step 3:

cluster,namespace,horizontalpodautoscaler,owner_team,workload_tier,cluster_hourly_cost_usd,expected_stabilize_sec,behavior_scaleup_period_sec
prod-eks-us-east-1,prod-payments,carts-api-hpa,payments-sre,production,85.50,300,60
lab-gke-dev,qa-load,checkout-hpa-qa,platform-test,dev,4.25,120,30

expected_stabilize_sec should reflect your platform standard for behavior.scaleUp.stabilizationWindowSeconds on tier-1 services. behavior_scaleup_period_sec should reflect the smallest periodSeconds in behavior.scaleUp.policies after GitOps review. When teams intentionally run aggressive scale-up for batch ingress, document that in workload_tier or add a suppression column in a local macro.

Validation searches before alert authoring:

index=k8s_metrics sourcetype=prometheus:scrape:metrics kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_current_replicas earliest=-30m latest=now

index=k8s_events sourcetype=kube:events HorizontalPodAutoscaler SuccessfulRescale earliest=-30m latest=now

Skew between scrapes and API events should remain under ninety seconds for meaningful correlation. If you run multiple clusters, normalize cluster or cluster_name fields at ingestion so the SPL cluster coalesce arm resolves consistently.

Security: redact internal queue names from alert emails when legal policy requires, while retaining hashed identifiers for correlation.

props.conf guidance: normalize __name__, value, namespace, and horizontalpodautoscaler fields onto indexed extractions where volume warrants acceleration experiments; keep coalesce ladders in SPL until extractions stabilize.

Cloud control planes: on EKS verify security groups still allow node to cluster IP reachability for metrics after landing-zone changes; on GKE verify managed Prometheus if you offloaded scrapes; on AKS verify managed Grafana agent label mapping still populates horizontalpodautoscaler.

Frequency: scrape interval, alert interval, and five-minute bin width must align mathematically; a fifteen-minute alert schedule pairs with earliest=-24h@h for daily flap reviews while five-minute schedules suit tighter operations.

Back-pressure: if kube-apiserver event watch disconnects, collector buffers should not grow unbounded; set retry and drop policies per vendor guidance.

Version pinning: record kube-state-metrics chart version in evidence packs quarterly.

Dashboard seeds: timechart of scale_events_1h by namespace, heatmap of ns_peak_scale_activity, and table of this UC output for executive summaries.

Summary index optional: materialize hourly snapshots of scale_events_24h into k8s_hpa_activity_summary when raw k8s_metrics scan costs dominate.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_3_2_11_hpa_scaling_activity with fifteen-minute schedule for fleet cost reviews or five-minute schedule for operations bridges, dispatch earliest=-24h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high for production-tier rows. Throttle duplicate pages per cluster, namespace, and horizontalpodautoscaler for twenty minutes unless severity escalates from medium to critical. Include scale_events_1h, scale_events_24h, flap_heavy, aggressive_policy_hint, successful_rescale_24h, cond_blob, and est_extra_cost_usd_24h in pager bodies so responders triage without opening Search.

Pipeline narrative for operators: the opening comment lists tunable indexes and burst gates. join with tstats against Performance provides a CIM-aligned tick count that proves Performance acceleration is warm for correlation overlays during audits; if acceleration is absent in a lab, the join still type=left preserves HPA rows. multisearch fans four parallel arms so a silent scrape on replica gauges does not blank the entire incident: kube-state-metrics current and desired replica series with streamstats per five-minute bucket to emit scale_evt tallies, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_condition pairs for ScalingActive, AbleToScale, and ScalingLimited, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_min_replicas, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_max_replicas, and kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_target_metric context, and kube:events SuccessfulRescale counts. coalesce ladders normalize cluster names across EKS, GKE, and AKS label habits. After fan-in stats by merge_key, inputlookup hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv enriches owner_team, workload_tier, FinOps cluster_hourly_cost_usd, expected_stabilize_sec, and behavior_scaleup_period_sec so aggressive_policy_hint can flag workloads whose declared Kubernetes behavior should throttle transitions more than observed rates imply. eventstats max(scale_events_24h) by namespace yields ns_peak_scale_activity for per-namespace flap heatmaps on dashboards. case implements severity: critical when flap_heavy fires on production tier, high when flap_heavy fires elsewhere or when aggressive_policy_hint pairs with sustained twenty-four hour activity, medium when raw transition counts exceed operational floors or when ScalingActive reads false while activity remains high. The closing table lists seventeen analyst-focused columns including target_metric_raw lines for spec context and est_extra_cost_usd_24h as a directional replica-hour surcharge estimate, not an invoice.

Paste-and-run SPL for alerts and dashboards must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-3.2.11 HPA scaling activity axis: replica and desired-replica transition rate, SuccessfulRescale corroboration, status conditions, spec context, burst gates (>=6 transitions in 1h or >=30 in 24h). Tunables: earliest=-24h@h latest=@m; bucket=5m; indexes k8s_metrics k8s_events")`
| eval uc_join="3211"
| join type=left uc_join [
| tstats count AS perf_lane_ticks FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE (nodename=Performance.CPU OR nodename=Performance.Memory) earliest=-24h@h latest=now
| eval uc_join="3211" ]
| fields - uc_join perf_lane_ticks
| multisearch [
    [ search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-24h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "horizontalpodautoscaler=\"(?<hpa_rx>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\"(?<ns_rx>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\s(?<mval>[0-9.eE+-]+)\s*$"
      | eval hpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(horizontalpodautoscaler, hpa_rx, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | where like(mn,"%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_current_replicas%") OR like(mn,"%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_desired_replicas%")
      | eval cur_r=if(like(mn,"%status_current_replicas%"), tonumber(mval, 10), null())
      | eval des_r=if(like(mn,"%status_desired_replicas%"), tonumber(mval, 10), null())
      | bin _time span=5m aligntime=@h
      | stats latest(cur_r) AS current_replicas latest(des_r) AS desired_replicas BY cluster namespace hpa _time
      | sort 0 cluster namespace hpa + _time
      | streamstats current=f global=f last(current_replicas) AS prev_cr last(desired_replicas) AS prev_dr BY cluster namespace hpa
      | eval scale_evt=if((isnotnull(prev_cr) AND current_replicas!=prev_cr) OR (isnotnull(prev_dr) AND desired_replicas!=prev_dr), 1, 0)
      | eval dir=case(isnull(prev_cr), "na", current_replicas>prev_cr OR (isnotnull(desired_replicas) AND isnotnull(prev_dr) AND desired_replicas>prev_dr), "up", current_replicas<prev_cr OR (isnotnull(desired_replicas) AND isnotnull(prev_dr) AND desired_replicas<prev_dr), "down", true(), "flat")
      | stats sum(scale_evt) AS scale_events_24h sum(eval(if(_time>=relative_time(now(),"-1h"), scale_evt, 0))) AS scale_events_1h values(dir) AS dir_turns BY cluster namespace hpa ]
    [ search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-24h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "horizontalpodautoscaler=\"(?<hpa_rx>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\"(?<ns_rx>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "condition=\"(?<cond_l>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "status=\"(?<stat_l>[^\"]+)\""
      | where like(mn,"%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_condition%")
      | eval hpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(horizontalpodautoscaler, hpa_rx, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval cond_pair=printf("%s=%s", cond_l, stat_l)
      | stats values(cond_pair) AS cond_values max(_time) AS cond_last_ts BY cluster namespace hpa ]
    [ search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-24h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "horizontalpodautoscaler=\"(?<hpa_rx>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\"(?<ns_rx>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\s(?<mval>[0-9.eE+-]+)\s*$"
      | where like(mn,"%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_min_replicas%") OR like(mn,"%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_max_replicas%") OR like(mn,"%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_spec_target_metric%")
      | eval hpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(horizontalpodautoscaler, hpa_rx, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval min_r=if(like(mn,"%spec_min_replicas%"), tonumber(mval, 10), null())
      | eval max_r=if(like(mn,"%spec_max_replicas%"), tonumber(mval, 10), null())
      | eval tgt_line=if(like(mn,"%spec_target_metric%"), _raw, null())
      | stats latest(min_r) AS min_replicas latest(max_r) AS max_replicas values(tgt_line) AS target_metric_raw BY cluster namespace hpa ]
    [ search (index=k8s_events OR index=k8s) sourcetype="kube:events" earliest=-24h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, source_cluster, ""))))
      | eval reason=trim(toString(coalesce(reason, Reason, "")))
      | eval kind=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject.kind, `involvedObject.kind`, ""))))
      | eval hpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject.name, involvedObject_name, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metadata.namespace, namespace, ""))))
      | where kind="horizontalpodautoscaler" AND reason="SuccessfulRescale"
      | stats count AS successful_rescale_24h BY cluster namespace hpa ]
| eval merge_key=cluster."|".namespace."|".hpa
| stats max(scale_events_24h) AS scale_events_24h max(scale_events_1h) AS scale_events_1h values(dir_turns) AS dir_turns values(cond_values) AS cond_values max(cond_last_ts) AS cond_last_ts max(min_replicas) AS min_replicas max(max_replicas) AS max_replicas values(target_metric_raw) AS target_metric_raw max(successful_rescale_24h) AS successful_rescale_24h BY merge_key
| rex field=merge_key "^(?<cluster>[^|]+)\|(?<namespace>[^|]+)\|(?<hpa>[^|]+)$"
| eval cond_blob=mvjoin(mvdedup(cond_values), " | ")
| eval flap_heavy=if(scale_events_1h>=6 OR scale_events_24h>=30, 1, 0)
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace hpa [
    | inputlookup hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(namespace)))
      | eval hpa=lower(trim(toString(horizontalpodautoscaler)))
      | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team, squad, platform_team, "")))
      | eval workload_tier=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(workload_tier, tier, env_tier, "dev"))))
      | eval cluster_hourly_cost_usd=tonumber(tostring(cluster_hourly_cost_usd), 10)
      | eval expected_stabilize_sec=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_stabilize_sec, "300")), 10)
      | eval behavior_scaleup_period_sec=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(behavior_scaleup_period_sec, "60")), 10)
      | fields cluster namespace hpa owner_team workload_tier cluster_hourly_cost_usd expected_stabilize_sec behavior_scaleup_period_sec ]
| fillnull value=0 flap_heavy successful_rescale_24h scale_events_1h scale_events_24h
| fillnull value="unassigned" owner_team
| fillnull value="dev" workload_tier
| fillnull value=0.0 cluster_hourly_cost_usd
| fillnull value=300 expected_stabilize_sec
| fillnull value=60 behavior_scaleup_period_sec
| eval est_extra_rep_hours=round(scale_events_24h*0.25, 3)
| eval est_extra_cost_usd_24h=round(est_extra_rep_hours*cluster_hourly_cost_usd, 2)
| eval aggressive_policy_hint=if(behavior_scaleup_period_sec<=15 AND scale_events_1h>=3, 1, if(expected_stabilize_sec<=60 AND scale_events_24h>=18, 1, 0))
| eventstats max(scale_events_24h) AS ns_peak_scale_activity BY namespace
| eval scaling_active_false=if(match(lower(cond_blob), "scalingactive=false"), 1, 0)
| eval severity=case(flap_heavy==1 AND match(workload_tier, "prod|production|gold|tier1|tier-1"), "critical", flap_heavy==1, "high", aggressive_policy_hint==1 AND scale_events_24h>=12, "high", scale_events_24h>=20 OR scale_events_1h>=5, "medium", scaling_active_false==1 AND scale_events_24h>=8, "medium", true(), "low")
| where severity IN ("critical","high","medium")
| table cluster namespace hpa owner_team workload_tier scale_events_1h scale_events_24h successful_rescale_24h flap_heavy aggressive_policy_hint min_replicas max_replicas cond_blob target_metric_raw ns_peak_scale_activity est_extra_cost_usd_24h severity
```

Alert actions: include cluster, namespace, horizontalpodautoscaler, severity, owner_team, scale_events_1h, scale_events_24h, and successful_rescale_24h in email or ITSI notable bodies. Provide a drilldown that runs index=k8s_events sourcetype=kube:events involvedObject.kind=HorizontalPodAutoscaler involvedObject.name=$row.hpa$ earliest=-24h latest=now. Provide a secondary drilldown for kube-state-metrics samples on the same object in k8s_metrics.

Performance: if Job Inspector warns on multisearch cost, split fleet dashboards into per-region saved searches or materialize hourly HPA activity snapshots.

Reliability: during kube-state-metrics upgrades expect brief gaps; require two consecutive intervals of missing metrics before paging scrape outages unless kube events still show SuccessfulRescale storms.

Governance: weekly CSV export of alert rows with lookup commit hash satisfies internal platform evidence when paired with kube-state-metrics image digest.

savedsearches.conf quantity thresholds should align with row counts from the table command; use alert.track=1 and suppress keys on cluster namespace horizontalpodautoscaler.

Closing Step 3 checklist: fenced SPL present, matches spl field, references hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv, explains tstats join purpose, documents multisearch arms, clarifies severity case ladder, and names notification fields.

Step 4 — Validate

Synthetic flap in lab: drive an HPA with intentionally tight averageUtilization against a workload whose CPU oscillates on a short period, hold the pattern until five-minute buckets show at least six transitions within one hour in Splunk, confirm SuccessfulRescale events appear for the same object, execute uc_3_2_11_hpa_scaling_activity, and expect flap_heavy equal to one with severity at least high when inventory marks production tier.

Synthetic cooldown compliance: widen behavior.scaleUp.stabilizationWindowSeconds and behavior.scaleDown.stabilizationWindowSeconds in Git, redeploy, wait two evaluation cycles, reload metrics, and confirm scale_events_1h falls below burst gates while load remains similar; document the before and after row for FinOps.

Synthetic negative path: remove oscillating load, confirm kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_current_replicas holds steady across twelve consecutive buckets, confirm SuccessfulRescale stops, and expect zero qualifying rows from the alert predicate across four consecutive runs for that HorizontalPodAutoscaler.

Field sanity: compare Splunk extracted namespace and horizontalpodautoscaler with kubectl get hpa -n and kubectl describe hpa output for the same minute.

RBAC: readers without k8s_metrics access must see zero rows.

Correlation: compare Splunk timestamps to kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.kind=HorizontalPodAutoscaler,reason=SuccessfulRescale for the same object.

Clock skew: verify NTP alignment between nodes, kube-apiserver, and Splunk indexers; skew beyond ninety seconds invalidates transition comparisons.

Lookup join: confirm hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv keys lower-case consistently with SPL eval lower(trim()) arms.

Cost sanity: when est_extra_cost_usd_24h spikes, validate cluster_hourly_cost_usd against your cloud cost allocation spreadsheet; the field is a planning nudge, not a bill.

Tear-down: delete lab stress workloads, restore behavior defaults, and confirm saved search result counts return to baseline.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — flap_heavy with alternating up and down dir_turns and healthy conditions: widen stabilization windows, increase metrics-server or adapter scrape alignment, reduce noise in external metrics, or raise minReplicas if traffic is genuinely bursty; attach target_metric_raw excerpts when present.

Case 2 — aggressive_policy_hint with short behavior_scaleup_period_sec in inventory versus Git drift: reconcile GitOps, enforce policy validation admission, and refuse merges that set periodSeconds below platform guardrails without FinOps approval.

Case 3 — high scale_events_24h but zero SuccessfulRescale: investigate kube:events delivery gaps, field extraction drift on involvedObject.name, or split-brain cluster labels before blaming the controller.

Case 4 — ScalingLimited true with modest transition counts: read kubectl describe for messages about maxReplicas; pair with UC-3.2.17 when dwell at ceiling dominates; pair with UC-3.2.46 when Pending pods imply node starvation.

Case 5 — ScalingActive false with elevated transitions in historical buckets: validate metrics API availability, custom.metrics.k8s.io aggregation health, and cloud metric bridges before retuning application code.

Case 6 — AbleToScale false during maintenance windows: confirm PodDisruptionBudget, deployment freezes, or admission denials; suppress known change records tied to inventory notes.

Case 7 — KEDA-driven HPAs with intentional burst semantics: cross-check ScaledObject cooldown, pause annotations, and idleReplicaCount; widen alert macros for event-driven namespaces that expect short-lived bursts.

Case 8 — SuccessfulRescale count far below metric-derived transitions: check events TTL, etcd compaction, or collector sampling; demand two independent signals before executive escalation.

Case 9 — Namespace-wide heatmap shows ns_peak_scale_activity concentrated in one tenant: engage that namespace owner for quota and noisy-neighbor review before cluster-wide throttle changes.

Case 10 — est_extra_cost_usd_24h alarms finance: replace the default 0.25 replica-hour heuristic in a local macro with your measured vCPU-minute model when FinOps publishes authoritative coefficients.

Case 11 — Symptoms resemble this UC but Deployment rollouts explain replica churn: pivot to UC-3.2.6 when kube_deployment_status_condition Progressing or Available breaches align with the same timeline.

Case 12 — Symptoms resemble this UC but nodes never arrive for new pods: open UC-3.2.46 when autoscaler logs and Pending timelines prove scale-out refusal while HPA keeps asking for width.

Dashboard hygiene: keep panels for scale_events_1h by namespace, SuccessfulRescale rate, and condition blobs with drilldowns to raw kube:events.

Evidence retention: weekly CSV exports of alert rows with hpa_scaling_activity_inventory.csv git hash satisfy internal audit samples when paired with kube-state-metrics chart version.

Governance: quarterly replay this search after kube-state-metrics upgrades because label rewriting rules change; update rex arms when Prometheus relabel configs move label names.

Closing checklist: five step headers use em dash punctuation as required; Step 3 includes fenced SPL matching the spl JSON field; multisearch covers replica transitions, conditions, spec context, and SuccessfulRescale; streamstats derives scale_evt; inputlookup enriches ownership and policy metadata; case assigns severity; closing table lists seventeen columns; monitoringType lists Performance and Capacity; cimModels lists Performance and Application_State.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how often the automatic replica scaler nudges instance counts up and down so we notice thrashing before bills jump or the service starts bouncing. We line that up with the cluster’s own success messages so we know the noise is real, not just a glitch in the charts.
hardware: Applies to Amazon EKS, Google GKE, Microsoft AKS, Red Hat OpenShift, VMware Tanzu, and self-managed Kubernetes where kube-state-metrics exposes HorizontalPodAutoscaler metrics and API events reach Splunk; x86_64 and Arm64 worker fleets are in scope when Prometheus text lines remain compatible.
